payload.answer: Use a dataset-style storage approach (index/summaries) rather than creating durable memory entries for every row.
payload.decision: Store as a structured/externally-referenced dataset (with indexing/summaries), rather than ingesting all rows verbatim as durable memory cards.

entity_resolution.reason: No specific real-world entities are provided beyond a generic CSV schema; the policy question is about storage format rather than entity facts.

payload.intent: Determine the appropriate storage strategy for a large CSV table (500 rows with repeated fields) within Brain’s personal memory system.

memory_cards.statement: For large CSV inputs (e.g., 500 rows with repeated id/name/value), prefer a structured dataset representation (index by id and/or canonicalize repeated values) with derived summaries, instead of storing every row verbatim as durable memory items.

details: Committed only a general storage policy; did not assert any row-specific factual content.

repair_options: If exact row-level retrieval is required, store rows in an external data store and keep only pointers/indices in Brain.
If only trends are needed, store aggregated statistics and a canonicalized set of unique (id, name, value) combinations.
If punctuation/formatting matters, preserve raw text as an attached artifact while extracting structured fields for memory.
